sections.content:
Perimeter and Area of 2D Shapes

Perimeter is the total length around the outside of a shape (measured in cm, m, etc.).

Area is the amount of space inside a shape (measured in cm², m², etc.).

Key formulas:
• Rectangle: Area = length × width (A = lw)
• Triangle: Area = ½ × base × height (A = ½bh)
• Circle: Area = π × r² ; Circumference = 2πr (use π ≈ 3.14159)
• Parallelogram: Area = base × height
• Trapezium: Area = ½(a + b) × h, where a and b are the parallel sides
🔑 Composite shapes: Break complex shapes into simpler ones (rectangles, triangles, semicircles), find the area of each part, then add (or subtract) them together.

Volume and Surface Area of 3D Shapes

Volume is the amount of space inside a 3D shape (measured in cm³, m³, etc.).

Key formulas:
• Rectangular prism (cuboid): V = l × w × h
• Cylinder: V = π × r² × h
• Triangle prism: V = area of triangle cross-section × length
• Cone: V = ⅓ × π × r² × h
• Sphere: V = 4/3 × π × r³

Surface area is the total area of all the faces of a 3D shape. For a rectangular prism: SA = 2(lw + lh + wh).

Unit Conversions

Length: 10 mm = 1 cm, 100 cm = 1 m, 1000 m = 1 km

Area: 1 cm² = 100 mm², 1 m² = 10 000 cm², 1 ha = 10 000 m²

Volume/Capacity: 1 cm³ = 1 mL, 1 L = 1000 mL = 1000 cm³, 1 m³ = 1000 L

questions.question: Find the area of a triangle with base 8 cm and height 5 cm
questions.explanation: Area of triangle = ½ × base × height = ½ × 8 × 5 = 20 cm².

questions.question: A circle has radius 7 cm. What is its area? (use π ≈ 3.14)
A rectangular prism has dimensions 4 cm × 3 cm × 6 cm. What is its volume?
A trapezium has parallel sides 5 cm and 9 cm, and a height of 4 cm. What is its area?
